name: Shanghai Spring Rolls
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine cabbage, celery, cilantro and seitan in a medium-size mixing bowl.
2. Set aside the mixture.
3. Sprinkle each spring roll with salt, pepper and a few drops of sesame oil.
4. Divide the vegetable mixture into 8 equal portions.
5. Spoon a portion about 2 inches from one corner of each spring rollskin.
6. Fold the corner over the filling, then fold in the side corners (similar to the three sealed sides of an envelope).
7. Roll towards the top to wrap completely.
8. Heat soybean oil to 350°F (175°C) in a deep saucepan or wok.
9. Carefully submerge the spring rolls into the hot oil and fry until golden, about 5 minutes. The rolls will float; turn them once during frying.
10. Remove with tongs and drain on absorbent paper.
11. Serve hot.
Per spring roll: 176 calories; 9g protein; 323mg sodium; 11g carbohydrates; 11g fat; 0mg cholesterol; 17mg calcium.
